audit_context: Full codebase pattern audit for cc-history-api — Phase 1 complete, constraining patterns for all future phases

applicable_patterns.pattern: Three-crate Cargo workspace: core (types+parser), store (SQLite+decomposition+sync), server (binary+CLI). Dependencies flow core->store->server. Workspace dependencies declared at root level.
Crate naming: claude-history-core, claude-history-store, claude-history (binary). Server crate produces binary via [[bin]] section.
Top-level JSONL record enum uses serde(tag = 'type') for discriminated union. 7 variants matching JSON type field values.
Overflow capture via #[serde(flatten)] HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> on outermost struct only. No overflow on RecordBase or enum variants.
#[serde(rename_all = 'camelCase')] on structs. Explicit serde(rename) for fields that break the camelCase transform.
Optional fields use #[serde(default)]. Polymorphic/opaque fields use serde_json::Value rather than typed enums when variant count is high.
ContentBlock enum uses serde(tag = 'type') with inline fields. No overflow on enum variants. Tool inputs/results as serde_json::Value.

Normalized table design with 13 tables. New tables for future phases must follow the same DDL conventions: TEXT for strings/timestamps, INTEGER for booleans (0/1), TEXT for JSON blobs, REFERENCES for foreign keys.
New DDL goes in numbered migration files (002_*.sql, 003_*.sql). Added to MIGRATIONS const array. run_migrations applies in order, idempotent.
Database init: WAL mode, 5s busy_timeout, synchronous=NORMAL, foreign_keys=ON. tokio-rusqlite conn.call() for synchronous SQLite operations.
INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotency. Duplicate decompositions produce 0 rows, not errors. All decomposition on &Transaction reference.
Per-type decompose_* functions: (1) upsert session, (2) insert event, (3) decompose content, (4) upsert agent, (5) log overflow.
Qualified record_type names for drift logging. INSERT OR IGNORE deduplication. Sample value truncation at 500 chars.
Incremental sync via byte-offset in sync_metadata. BATCH_SIZE=1000 chunk processing. Per-batch transaction commits with atomic metadata updates.
Line-by-line BufReader parsing for byte-offset tracking. Malformed lines -> ParseWarning, never halt. ParseError for file-level I/O only.
Per-crate thiserror error types. SchemaError maps via ToSqlConversionFailure for tokio-rusqlite conn.call() compatibility.
clap derive CLI with subcommand enum. #[tokio::main] async. tracing to stderr, output to stdout. ExitCode return.
Path resolution priority: CLI arg > env var > $HOME default.
Inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests. Descriptive /// comments. Helper functions per module. Store tests use in-memory SQLite.
Async tests via #[tokio::test]. tempfile for temp dirs. Exact row count + field value verification. Idempotency explicitly tested.
Module-level //! doc comments. Struct/function doc comments explain rationale. Requirement IDs cited. Empirical percentages where known.
Planning artifacts: PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md, config.json in .planning/
Phase directories with RESEARCH, PLAN, execution-results, SUMMARY, VERIFICATION files. PLAN.md YAML frontmatter.
Dev-dependencies per crate, not at workspace level. tempfile for tests, tokio for async tests.
Flat pub mod list in lib.rs. No re-exports or facades. Consumers import from specific module paths.
ToolUse blocks produce dual inserts: message_content + tool_executions. Both INSERT OR IGNORE.
Index naming: idx_{table}_{column}. Created on high-cardinality lookup columns.

anti_patterns.violation: Adding serde(flatten) overflow HashMap to RecordBase or any shared inner struct
anti_patterns.correct: Overflow HashMap goes on outermost containing struct only (e.g., UserRecord, AssistantRecord) — one overflow per struct hierarchy path
anti_patterns.reason: Multiple serde(flatten) on nested structs causes ambiguity where serde cannot determine which HashMap should capture unknown fields. Documented in STATE.md decision [01-02].

anti_patterns.violation: Halting JSONL parsing on a malformed line
anti_patterns.correct: Malformed lines produce ParseWarning entries; parsing continues to the next line. ParseError is for file-level I/O failures only.
anti_patterns.reason: Claude Code may be actively writing to a file, producing partial final lines. A single corrupted line must not prevent ingesting thousands of valid records. Requirement CORE-07.

anti_patterns.violation: Using serde StreamDeserializer instead of line-by-line BufReader parsing
anti_patterns.correct: Parse line-by-line with BufReader::lines() to enable byte-offset tracking for incremental sync
anti_patterns.reason: StreamDeserializer cannot provide byte offsets for individual records, which are required for sync_metadata tracking. Requirement SYNC-01.

anti_patterns.violation: Creating separate test files instead of inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests
anti_patterns.correct: Tests live as #[cfg(test)] mod tests within the same source file they test
anti_patterns.reason: Established project convention. All 12+ test modules in the codebase follow this pattern. Keeps test helpers close to the code they test.

anti_patterns.violation: Using UPDATE for session rows when session already exists
anti_patterns.correct: INSERT OR IGNORE — first record seen creates the row, duplicates are no-ops
anti_patterns.reason: Session metadata varies across records within the same session. First-seen value is most useful for provenance. UPDATE would overwrite first-seen data with later data.

anti_patterns.violation: Adding new tables directly to 001_initial.sql or modifying existing migration files
anti_patterns.correct: New DDL goes in new numbered migration files (002_*.sql, etc.) added to the MIGRATIONS array in schema.rs
anti_patterns.reason: Existing databases already have 001_initial.sql applied. Modifying it would not affect existing databases. Migrations are immutable once applied.

anti_patterns.violation: Placing new dependencies at workspace level when only one crate needs them
anti_patterns.correct: Add dependencies to the specific crate's Cargo.toml. Only dependencies shared by multiple crates belong in workspace.dependencies
anti_patterns.reason: Keeps dependency scope minimal and compilation times down. Phase 2 additions (e.g., axum) should go in server crate only until needed elsewhere.

anti_patterns.violation: Using raw rusqlite Connection in async code paths
anti_patterns.reason: rusqlite Connection is !Send. tokio-rusqlite wraps it in a dedicated thread. All async code must use the conn.call() bridge pattern.

anti_patterns.correct: tracing logs go to stderr. Human-readable or machine-parseable output goes to stdout.
anti_patterns.reason: Allows piping stdout output through jq or other tools without tracing noise. Established in main.rs with .with_writer(std::io::stderr).

anti_patterns.violation: Using unwrap() or expect() in library crate code (core, store)
anti_patterns.correct: Return Result with appropriate error types. unwrap/expect only in test code and CLI binary.
anti_patterns.reason: Library crates must propagate errors to callers. Only the binary entry point and test assertions should panic.
